fix: Validate missing <cwd-path> argument in test-codemod server script
prs.body:
Adds a simple validation to scripts/test-codemod/server.js to handle the case where the required <cwd-path> argument is missing. This prevents unclear spawn() errors and provides a clear usage message instead.

Testing Instructions:

Run node scripts/test-codemod/server.js → should show a clear missing-argument error.
Run with a valid path → script should behave normally.

Fixes #11165

feat(i18n): complete and update full Hindi (hi) translation with missing keys
prs.body: This PR completes the Hindi (hi) translation for DrawDB by adding all missing keys, updating outdated strings, and ensuring full consistency with the English source (en) file. What’s Included: - Added all missing Hindi translations for newly introduced i18n keys - Updated existing translations for clarity and natural phrasing - Ensured placeholders like {{tableName}}, {{fieldName}}, etc. remain unchanged - Verified consistency with the English base file - Checked UI rendering to confirm correct loading of new keys Keys Added / Updated: - Includes keys such as (but not limited to): - upload_file - insert_sql - export_saved_data - upload_sql_to_generate_diagrams - dbml_view, tab_view - bulk_update, multiselect - Version history + timeline keys - Debug/coordinate system keys - Enum + array + relationship label keys UI Screenshot: Checklist: - Added missing translation keys - Verified translations in the UI - Ensured JSON structure follows project conventions - Performed full completeness check against English file Fixes #740

prs.title: feat(i18n): complete missing Persian (fa-IR) translations and fill empty localization fields
prs.body:
This PR fills missing translations in the Persian (fa-IR) locale file. Several keys were previously empty, resulting in blank UI labels. All missing fields have now been accurately translated. Modifier keys (Ctrl, Shift, etc.) were intentionally kept in English. The JSON structure has been validated and remains consistent with existing fa-IR terminology.

Completed translations include:

- polygon.breakPolygon, polygon.convertToPolygon
- tab, shapeSwitch
- Library/search placeholders
- search.frames, search.texts
- toolBar.lasso, toolBar.convertElementType etc

Missing values caused broken UI rendering for Persian-speaking users.
This update restores full i18n coverage and significantly improves clarity, accessibility, and overall UX.

Fixes #10381

prs.title: feat(migrate): support --sql for prisma db push
prs.body:
This PR introduces support for generating typed SQL as part of prisma db push.
When the --sql flag is provided, or when typedSql is enabled in prisma.config.ts, the CLI will automatically invoke prisma generate --sql after a successful push.
This aligns the behavior of db push with prisma generate and removes the need for a second manual step.

Implementation Details-

Command behavior: 
- Extended DbPush to accept the --sql flag.
- After db push completes, the CLI triggers prisma generate --sql.
- Relevant flags (--schema, --config, telemetry) are forwarded.
- Uses execaNode to spawn the generator CLI.
This keeps the change isolated and avoids cross-package runtime dependencies.

Testing:
- Added DbPush.generate.test.ts to verify that --sql correctly results in a downstream call to the generator.
- Test uses virtual Jest mocks consistent with Prisma’s existing test infra.

Unit test:
```
pnpm --filter @prisma/migrate exec -- npx jest packages/migrate/src/__tests__/DbPush.generate.test.ts --runInBand --verbose

```

Fixes #28524

prs.title: fix(cli): avoid unwrap panic when resolving npm_execpath on Windows
prs.body:
This PR fixes a panic that occurred during tauri android init when npm_execpath pointed to a path where file_stem() returned None—a situation that happens on some Windows setups and when using Bun.

Previously, the code called:
```
file_stem().unwrap().to_os_string()
```
If file_stem() returned None, the CLI crashed with an unwrap() panic.

What this PR changes-

This replaces the unsafe unwrap() with a safe fallback chain:
1. file_stem()
2. Or file_name()
3. Or the original npm_execpath
This ensures that the CLI never panics due to unexpected or non-standard npm/bun paths.

Why this is correct-

- Handles Windows-specific cases where file_stem() legitimately returns None.
- Makes the manager detection code fully panic-safe across all platforms.
- Does not change higher-level behavior or command semantics.
- Fixes the root cause of the crash without introducing breaking changes.

Verification-

- Rebuilt tauri-cli
- Symlinked the binary to node and set npm_execpath="/" to simulate the failing scenario
- Confirmed the CLI no longer panics and proceeds normally

Fixes #14472

prs.title: Add kill-tree helper and runtime sidecar PID registry
prs.body:
Adds a best-effort process-tree killer and a lightweight runtime sidecar PID registry, ensuring that descendant processes spawned by sidecars are properly terminated when their parent sidecar is killed.

This resolves the core issue described in #14360 by introducing both API and runtime cleanup mechanisms for sidecar process management.

Details:

- tauri::process::kill_process_tree(pid: u32) — POSIX shell & PowerShell based, best-effort.
- Sidecar PID registry via AppHandle::register_sidecar(pid) / unregister_sidecar(pid) for cleanup on exit.
- App::cleanup_before_exit() drains registry and calls the kill helper.
- CLI dev-run now attempts process-tree cleanup when stopping dev children.

Files Changed:

- process.rs: Added kill_process_tree() (POSIX + PowerShell).
- mod.rs: Added sidecar PID tracking + helper methods.
- app.rs: Added register/unregister APIs; updated cleanup.
- desktop.rs: Added kill-tree logic in dev-run path.
- CHANGELOG.md / PR_DRAFT.md: Updated with notes.

Testing:

- [ ✅ ] cargo test -p tauri, all tests passed.
- [ ✅ ] Manual: verified registry and cleanup invoke kill helper.
- Runtime cleanup is opt-in:
   Call app_handle.register_sidecar(child.id() as u32) after spawning.

Fixes #14360

prs.title: docs(blog): replace deprecated loadWasm examples with engine usage
prs.body:
Fix documentation that incorrectly suggested loadWasm was exported from the top-level shiki package.
The examples now reference the correct engine package and show usage with createOnigurumaEngine, which replaces the deprecated loadWasm field on createHighlighterCore.

What This PR Changes

Replaces outdated:
```
import { loadWasm } from 'shiki'
```
with the correct:

```
import { loadWasm } from 'shiki/engine/oniguruma'
```

Updates examples to use:
```
engine: createOnigurumaEngine(() => import('shiki/wasm'))
```
instead of the removed loadWasm API.
Removes all mentions of the deprecated loadWasm field from Cloudflare Worker and Core examples.

Files Changed

- docs/v2.md
- docs/v3.md

Linked Issue
Fixes: #1113 — No longer exists loadWasm function declaration in shiki/core

prs.title: fix(monaco): apply last theme by default to fix
prs.body:
This PR fixes an issue where the **last theme was not applied correctly** in Shiki when switching themes.  
The problem occurred due to improper handling of theme state in the Monaco integration. This ensures that the last selected theme is now consistently applied.

### Linked Issues
Fixes #1079

### Additional context
- Updated test cases in `test-shiki.ts` to cover theme application.  
- Ensured ESLint rules pass (manual fixes applied).  
- Minor adjustments in `bundle-factory.ts` to handle theme state correctly.

prs.title: Fix: Prevent invalid language from corrupting singleton highlighter in codeToHtml
prs.body:
This PR fixes the issue where calling codeToHtml with an invalid language on the first call causes all future calls to fail, even with valid languages. The root cause was that the singleton highlighter (makeSingletonHighlighter) was being initialized with languages directly from the first call.
If that initial call contained an unsupported language, the highlighter became permanently invalid.

## Fix:

Initialize the highlighter with an empty langs array.
Then dynamically load provided languages using loadLanguage(...).
This ensures that invalid languages fail locally without corrupting the shared highlighter.

## Linked Issues
Fixes #1089

## Additional context

This fix ensures:
Invalid language calls only affect the current invocation.
Future valid calls succeed as expected.
Tested by reproducing the issue sequence (invalid → valid → valid) where all calls now behave correctly.

prs.title: Fix: Allow mock.property() to work with process.env variables

labels.name: needs-ci
labels.description: PRs that need a full CI run.

labels.name: test_runner
labels.description: Issues and PRs related to the test runner subsystem.

prs.body:
### Fix: Allow `mock.property()` to work with `process.env` variables

#### Description

This PR fixes a `TypeError` that occurs when using `mock.property()` from the `node:test` module on `process.env` properties.

**Error before:**
TypeError: 'process.env' does not accept an accessor(getter/setter) descriptor

**Fix:**  
When the target is `process.env`, `mock.property()` now performs a direct value assignment instead of using an accessor descriptor.  
This ensures environment variables can be safely mocked and restored without throwing errors.

#### Reproduction Steps

```js
import { mock } from "node:test";

console.log(Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(process.env, "PATH"));
mock.property(process.env, "PATH", "foo");
console.log(process.env.PATH);
```

**With this fix:**
{ value: '/usr/local/bin:...', writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true }
foo

**Tests Added:**  
Added a  new test file:
test/parallel/test-env-mock-process-env.js

The test validates:
- The mock operation on process.env variables works correctly.
- The environment variable can be restored safely.

Example output:
Mocked process.env.TEST_ENV successfully
Restored original process.env.TEST_ENV

Checklist:
- [✅]  Includes test coverage for the change.
- [✅]  make -j4 test passes locally.
- [✅]  No documentation changes required.
- [✅]  Commit message follows Node.js guidelines.

Developer’s Certificate of Origin 1.1

By making a contribution to this project, I certify that:

(a) The contribution was created in whole or in part by me and I
have the right to submit it under the open source license indicated in the file; or
(b) The contribution is based upon previous work that, to the best of my knowledge, is covered under an appropriate open source license; or
(c) The contribution was provided directly to me by another person who certified (a), (b), or (c); and
(d) I understand and agree that this project and the contribution are public and that a record of the contribution (including my sign-off) is maintained indefinitely.

fixes #60486

prs.title: research(xcode): added import PoC and extraction script
prs.body:
Added a Markdown file research/xcode/import-snippets.md detailing the proof of concept for a new command to import snippets into XCode.

Answered key questions:

XCode user snippets are stored at:
~/Library/Developer/Xcode/UserData/CodeSnippets/
Each XCode snippet requires its own .codesnippet file.
Included a sample script to read a snippet’s title, body, description, and prefix trigger and log them to the console.

Why:
This PoC helps automate the process of importing snippets into XCode, making snippet management easier for developers.

Testing:
Verified the script correctly extracts and logs snippet details locally.

Notes:
Related to the “Export/Import Snippets to other IDEs” discussion.
Issue #27

prs.title: Add PoC for exporting snippets to Xcode
prs.body:
This PR adds a proof-of-concept for exporting code snippets to Xcode. 

Changes include:
- `research/xcode/export-to-xcode.md` documentation file explaining:
  - Xcode user snippet path (`~/Library/Developer/Xcode/UserData/CodeSnippets/`)
  - Requirement of one .codesnippet file per snippet
  - Important plist keys (identifier, title, summary, contents, language, prefix, scopes, userSnippet)
- Node.js PoC script that:
  - Generates valid `.codesnippet` files
  - Accepts language, id, title/summary, prefix, and snippet body
  - Uses UUIDs for missing IDs and safely escapes XML content
- Usage examples and notes on placeholders, language identifiers, and scopes

Issue #28

prs.title: Added Debugger Support with ExecutionContext and Web API Integration and implemented bot's suggestions with synced main
prs.body:
This pull request introduces the debugging functionality into the KidCode project by integrating an ExecutionContext for managing program execution state, setting breakpoints, and controlling program flow (pause, resume, step, terminate).

Key Changes:

Added ExecutionContext class in the kidcode-web module to handle debugger state and controls.
Implemented DebuggerTest class to demonstrate breakpoint handling and debugger control flow.
Created DebuggerController REST API endpoints (/pause, /resume, /step, /terminate) to allow external control of program execution through HTTP calls.
Updated kidcode-web/pom.xml to include dependency on kidcode-core and Spring Boot starter web.
Fixed package import and type mismatches between core and web modules to ensure proper interaction.
Verified full build success and ability to run Spring Boot web application serving debugger endpoints.
How to Test:

Run the DebuggerTest main method to verify breakpoint pause and resume functionality.
Start the web application using mvn -pl kidcode-web spring-boot:run.
Use HTTP POST requests to the /debugger endpoints to pause, resume, step, or terminate execution.
Confirm expected behavior via logs and program state changes.
Notes:
This lays the groundwork for adding a full debugger UI or integration with IDEs.
Future work may include enhanced breakpoint management and state inspection APIs.

Screenshot:
![Uploading Screenshot 2025-10-14 at 2.23.12 PM.png…]()


Fixes https://github.com/Sansi-28/KidCode/issues/16

Summary by CodeRabbit
New Features

Interactive debugging: pause, resume, step through, and terminate program execution.
Breakpoint support by line number to halt execution at specific points.
HTTP endpoints to control debugging actions remotely.
Improvements

Clearer runtime feedback with line-aware statements for easier troubleshooting.
Smoother execution control flow to better support pausing and stepping during runs.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Remote debugger endpoints to pause, resume, step, and terminate execution
  * Breakpoint support that stops execution at specified source lines
  * Stepping mode pauses after each statement for interactive inspection

* **Bug Fixes / Improvements**
  * Improved source-line tracking for more accurate breakpoints
  * Parser more resilient to malformed input to avoid hanging
  * Execution can be paused/resumed safely across threads

* **Tests**
  * Added a small debug-run utility demonstrating pause/step/resume flow
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: feat: Added shell snippet command handlers with runImmediately support
prs.body:
This PR implements the command handlers for shell snippets in Snippet Studio. It covers the following features:

- Create a new shell snippet using an input box and set the runImmediately property.
- Edit and delete existing shell snippets.
- Run a shell snippet in the VS Code terminal, respecting the runImmediately flag.
- Refresh the shell snippets tree view after any update.
- Store snippets in workspace configuration (`snippetstudio.shell.snippets`).

This addresses the tasks described in issue #34 and ensures seamless execution of shell snippets for both local and global configurations.

Shell Snippet Schema:
{
  "command": "string",
  "runImmediately": "boolean"
}

Fixes Issue #34 
#32

prs.title: Added command handler to define and update shell snippets
prs.body:
This PR implements the feature to define and update shell command snippets in the Snippet Studio extension, addressing the tasks outlined in issue #32.

Changes included:

- Added defineShellSnippet() command to get a new shell command via showInputBox.
- Reads the current shell snippets from local or global snippetstudio.shell.snippets configuration.
- Updates the target shell snippet object or adds a new snippet with command and runImmediately properties.
- Saves the updated snippets back to workspace or global configuration.
- Refreshes the shell snippets tree view automatically.
- Ensures commands can be either pasted into the terminal or run immediately, supporting local or global scope.

Fixes #32

prs.title: Added Pause and Resume Feature to Stories
prs.body:
This PR introduces the ability to pause and resume stories for better user control and interaction.

---

####  Features Implemented:

* `pauseStory()` and `resumeStory()` methods added to core component.
* Spacebar key toggles between pause and play.
* Long-press (hold) pauses story; releasing resumes it.
* Video playback and progress bar are synced with pause/resume.
* Refactored logic to prevent progress updates during buffering or pause state.

---

####  Why This Matters:

* Gives users more control over how they consume stories.
* Improves accessibility for keyboard users.
* Lays foundation for advanced playback controls in future (e.g., play/pause button).

---

####  How to Test:

1. Run the app.
2. Try pressing **spacebar** to pause/resume a story.
3. **Hold down** mouse/finger on a story — it should pause.
4. **Release** — it should resume.
5. Test with both image and video stories.



## Checklist before requesting a review
- [ ✅] I have performed a self-review of my code.
- [✅ ] Followed the repository's [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/Gauravdarkslayer/ngx-stories/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ✅] I ran the app and tested it locally to verify that it works as expected.
- [✅ ] I have starred the repository.

Fixes #13 


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

* **Documentation**

prs.title: Added a standardized pull request template
prs.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a standardized pull request template to guide contributors in structuring their PRs effectively. 

---

## Motivation

Currently, the repository does not have any PR template. Adding a pr template will help ensure clarity, consistency, and completeness in future contributions.

---

## Changes

- I've added `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` containing sections for summary, motivation, changes, testing, checklist, and related issues. Happy to make changes if needed, please let me know.

---

## Testing

The template will render automatically in new pull requests once this PR is merged into the default branch and can be tested thereafter.

Fixes #186

prs.title: Added required permissions for Release Drafter workflow to function properly
prs.body:
This PR adds the required permissions for the Release Drafter workflow to function properly with Dependabot PRs.
with the help of the docs, I observed that the workflow needed:

- contents: write
- pull-requests: write

In order to ensure Release Drafter can properly draft releases for Dependabot PRs and be manually triggered if needed,
I also added (workflow_dispatch) to enable manual triggering of the workflow for testing purposes.

### Testing done

- YAML syntax validated locally.
- Changes pushed to a separate branch: fix/release-drafter-permissions-clean.
- Manual workflow run not tested due to permission limitations on forked repositories 

### Submitter checklist

[x]  Opened from a feature branch (fix/release-drafter-permissions-clean)
[x]  PR title clearly reflects the changelog purpose
[x]  Described the changes clearly
[x]  Verified file structure and syntax
[x]  Change is small and scoped properly

Fixes #84

prs.title: Add React-Based Note Sharing WebView to VS Code Extension
prs.body:
This PR integrates a fully functional note-sharing interface into the VS Code extension using React. It allows users to write, save, and clear notes directly from a custom WebView panel in VS Code.

🚀 What’s Implemented:

VS Code Extension (extension.js):

- Registers a command: "extension.showReactWebview" to open a WebView.
- Injects the bundled React app (bundle.js) inside a custom HTML shell.
- WebView loads the React UI and allows interaction without leaving VS Code.
- React Note-Taking UI (src/App.js):
- Users can write text in a styled <textarea>.
- Includes "💾 Save Note" and "🧹 Clear" buttons.
- Displays the saved note in a readable format below the input.
- proper in depth README.md file with all the instructions possible for a newbie.

Frontend Integration (src/index.js):

1. Bootstraps the App using ReactDOM.createRoot.

Styling and UI:

1. Inline styles using a styles object for layout, spacing, and colors.
2. Emoji-enhanced UI to improve clarity and user engagement.

Build System:

1. Configured with webpack and babel to bundle React JSX.
2. Outputs compiled bundle.js used by the WebView.

🧪 How to Test:

1. Run npm install
2. Run npm run build to generate dist/bundle.js
3. Open project in VS Code and press F5 to launch the Extension Development Host
4. Run the command: "Show React Webview"
5.  You should see the note editor interface

📦 Tech Stack & Tools:

1. Frontend: React (useState, inline CSS styling)
2. Build: Webpack, Babel (@babel/preset-react, babel-loader)
3. Editor Integration: VS Code WebView API

prs.title: Add documentation for Comment Ops and Crowdin configurations
prs.body:
### Summary

This pull request updates the `README.md` to include documentation for two configuration files that already exist in the repository but were not listed under the available global configurations:

- `.github/comment-ops.yml`: Enables GitHub commands like `/reviewer` through the [github-comment-ops](https://github.com/timja/github-comment-ops) app. Some repositories, like [jenkinsci/jenkins](https://github.com/jenkinsci/jenkins/blob/master/.github/comment-ops.yml), also enable additional commands like automatic labeling.

- `workflow-templates/crowdin.yml`: Defines translation workflow integration with Crowdin. Additional details are provided in the [Jenkins Crowdin documentation](https://www.jenkins.io/doc/developer/crowdin/).

These updates aim to improve clarity around which global configurations are available to Jenkins project repositories.

---

### Testing done

- Manually verified the Markdown formatting and all links.
- No code or logic was modified, so no automated testing was required.

---

### Submitter checklist
- [x] Made changes from a feature branch
- [x] PR title reflects the change made
- [x] Clearly described the change
- [x] No related GitHub issues or PRs (self-contained update)
- [x] No test applicable for a documentation-only update

Fixes #149

prs.title: Applied Prettier Formatting and Configured .prettierrc.json and .prettierignore
prs.body:
## Description

-To maintain a uniform code style and enhance code quality across the project, Prettier formatting scripts have been added to package.json. These scripts enable developers to easily format code and check for adherence to predefined formatting rules. Additionally, a .prettierrc file has been created to define project-specific Prettier configuration rules, ensuring consistency and reducing the need for manual adjustments.

## Changes Made

### Added the necessary scripts in package.json:

Scripts were added to package.json to enable automatic code formatting and validation. One script formats all project files, while the other checks for adherence to Prettier rules without making changes.

### Created a .prettierrc.json and prettierignore file to define custom Prettier configuration settings.
----

## How It Works

npm run format → Automatically formats all files in the project according to Prettier rules.

npm run format:check → Checks if files follow Prettier formatting without making any changes.

.prettierrc.json → Stores the project's Prettier configuration to maintain consistent formatting across the codebase.

.prettierignore → Specifies files and directories that should be ignored by Prettier, preventing unnecessary formatting of certain files like build outputs, dependencies, or specific project files.

## Benefits

1. Consistent Code Formatting – Ensures uniform code style across the project, improving readability.
2. Improved Collaboration – Reduces formatting-related conflicts in PR reviews.
3. Better Maintainability – Clean and structured code makes future modifications easier.
4. Automated Formatting – Saves time by automatically formatting files, reducing manual effort.
5. Adheres to Best Practices – Enforces coding standards, making the codebase more professional.

## Fixes #235

prs.title: Applied Prettier Formatting and Configured .prettierrc.json
prs.body:
## Description

-To maintain a uniform code style and enhance code quality across the project, Prettier formatting scripts have been added to package.json. These scripts enable developers to easily format code and check for adherence to predefined formatting rules. Additionally, a .prettierrc file has been created to define project-specific Prettier configuration rules, ensuring consistency and reducing the need for manual adjustments.

## Changes Made

### Added the following scripts in package.json:

"scripts": {
  "format": "prettier --write .",
  "format:check": "prettier --check ."
}

### Created a .prettierrc file to define custom Prettier configuration settings.

## How It Works

npm run format → Automatically formats all files in the project according to Prettier rules.

npm run format:check → Checks if files follow Prettier formatting without making any changes.

.prettierrc → Stores the project's Prettier configuration to maintain consistent formatting across the codebase.

## Benefits

Consistent Code Formatting – Ensures uniform code style across the project, improving readability.
Improved Collaboration – Reduces formatting-related conflicts in PR reviews.
Better Maintainability – Clean and structured code makes future modifications easier.
Automated Formatting – Saves time by automatically formatting files, reducing manual effort.
Adheres to Best Practices – Enforces coding standards, making the codebase more professional.

## Fixes #235

Alert displays when no input is provided. 2. Loading does not proceed without input. 3. Normal functionality works seamlessly when input is valid. ## Before changes: ## After changes: ### Fixes #142

prs.title: Fix Setup Instructions for Service Account File in README.md
prs.body:
This pull request addresses an issue where users were unable to correctly set up the service account file before starting the server. The instructions for configuring the service account file were originally placed later in the documentation, leading to confusion and potential errors during the setup process.

Issue Resolved:
Issue: Users encountered difficulties setting up the service account file because the required steps were only mentioned after the server startup instructions.
Fix: I have updated the documentation to move the service account file setup instructions to appear before the server startup steps, ensuring a smoother setup experience.
Changes Made:
Moved the service account setup instructions to the appropriate position in the documentation, before the server start section.
Added clarity to the instructions to ensure users understand the importance of configuring the service account file beforehand.
Testing and Verification:
Verified that the new sequence of instructions eliminates confusion and allows users to complete the setup without issues.
Tested the updated process by following the steps to confirm the service starts correctly after configuring the service account.
Impact:
This change improves the usability of the documentation and prevents setup errors, making it easier for new users to get started.

issues.title: 📚 Docs: Improve clarity by adding arg validation note for test-codemod server script
issues.body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

The internal script at `scripts/test-codemod/server.js` currently crashes
with confusing spawn() errors if a required <cwd-path> argument is
missing.

I would like to improve the documentation (or inline script comments) to
reflect that this script requires a <cwd-path> parameter, and optionally
document a simple validation step.

This helps new contributors understand how to run codemod tests without
running into unclear ENOENT / ENOTDIR errors.


### Is there any context that might help us understand?

Running the script without providing a path:

    node scripts/test-codemod/server.js

causes spawn("pnpm", ["run", "start"], { cwd: undefined }) which results
in errors such as:

    Error: spawn pnpm ENOENT
    Error: spawn ENOTDIR

Adding a short note or guidance in the docs or a validation check in the
script helps prevent confusion for contributors who run this script.


### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

This relates to repo-internal developer tooling and does not have a specific docs page.  Closest relevant section: https://turbo.build/repo/docs/contributing

issues.title: Improve and Complete Hindi (hi) Translations for DrawDB UI
issues.body:
The Hindi (hi) translation file is partially complete and several keys from the English (en) source are missing. These missing entries cause fallback to English in parts of the UI.

Missing / incomplete keys include (not limited to):

- upload_fileinsert_sql
- export_saved_data
- dbml_view, tab_view
- Timeline + versions keys
- Bulk update + multiselect keys
- Relationship label / coordinate system keys


Tasks:

- Add all missing Hindi translation keys.
- Improve consistency with natural Hindi phrasing.
- Keep variable placeholders (e.g., {{tableName}}) unchanged.
- Perform a completeness check against the English source file before submitting the PR.

Checklist:

[ ] -  Add missing keys
[ ] -  Verify all Hindi translations
[ ] -  Ensure formatting and structure match project standards

issues.title: Missing Persian translations in fa-IR.json
issues.body:
While auditing the Persian (fa-IR) localization file, I identified multiple keys with empty or null translation values, resulting in incomplete UI rendering for Persian users. These gaps directly impact the internationalization integrity, causing fallback failures and inconsistent UX across various components of the application. The affected keys include:

- polygon.breakPolygon
- polygon.convertToPolygon
- tab
- shapeSwitch
- library.search.inputPlaceholder
- library.search.heading
- library.search.noResults
- library.search.clearSearch
- search.frames
- search.texts
- toolBar.lasso
- toolBar.convertElementType

Additionally, there are other untranslated or partially translated entries discovered during a full audit of the locale file. I would like to contribute a patch to address these missing translations and ensure full coverage of the fa-IR locale.

issues.title: Design a 3D-Style Logo for VS Code Extension
issues.body:
We are looking for a 3D-style logo for our VS Code extension. The logo should be unique, modern, and visually represent the purpose of the extension.

Style: 3D or isometric (with subtle depth, shadows, or gradient effects).

Format:
.svg (preferred)
.png (at least 128x128px)

Design: Clean, professional, techy; must look good on both dark and light themes.

Originality: The design should be your own and free to use for open-source/public projects.

What to Include in Your PR:
Logo file(s) added to:
assets/logo/ folder

One preview image (PNG or JPG) added to the same folder

issues.title: Refactor App.js into Reusable Components
issues.body: The current App.js file contains a lot of logic and UI elements bundled together, which makes it harder to manage, scale, and test. To improve the structure and readability of the codebase, we should break down App.js into smaller, reusable functional components.

issues.title: Commit Message Helper
issues.body:
Send keywords → return a clean commit message.

Backend API: POST /commit-helper
Example input: "added login UI"
Response: "feat(ui): add login interface"

Use: Use in Git sidebar or as quick command

issues.title: Clean My Folder
issues.body:
Scan a folder for unused/temporary files.

Backend API:
GET /cleanup-suggestions → Returns files like .log, .tmp, __pycache__

Use: Show delete suggestions → allow user to delete files.

issues.title: VS Code Usage Timer
issues.body:
Track how long user spends coding in each session.

Backend APIs:
POST /start → Start timer
POST /stop → Stop timer & save duration
GET /stats → Show total hours

Use: Auto log on extension start/stop, show graph in WebView.

issues.title: Internet Check API
issues.body:
Feature: Show if internet is connected.

Backend API:
GET /status → Return "Online" or "Offline"

Use:
Show status dot or banner in UI

issues.title: Implement Prettier Formatting Scripts for Code Consistency
issues.body:
# Description
 To ensure a consistent code style and enhance code quality across the project, Prettier formatting scripts should be added to package.json. These scripts will allow developers to effortlessly format code and verify compliance with predefined formatting rules. Additionally, a .prettierrc file should be created to define project-specific Prettier configurations, eliminating the need for manual adjustments.

issues.title: Infinite Loop risk when all riddles are used in riddles.py
issues.body: The assign_riddle() function does not correctly reset used_riddles when all available riddles have been attempted. This results in an empty list for random.choice(), leading to an IndexError and can lead to an infinite loop.

issues.title: Update Webpage Logo to Transparent Version for Consistency with extension
issues.body:
## Description:
The current logo on the webpage uses a non-transparent image, which leads to visual inconsistency, especially against varying background colors. The project extension already includes a transparent version of the logo, which ensures better alignment with the website’s design and enhances overall visual uniformity. Updating the logo to its transparent version on the webpage will provide several benefits:

### Improved Visual Consistency: 

A transparent logo will blend seamlessly with page's dark themed background, making the design appear more polished and cohesive.
### Professional Appearance:

 A transparent logo is the standard in modern web design, ensuring the website looks more professional and well-designed.
### Better User Experience: The transparent logo will not clash with background images or sections, improving the overall aesthetic and readability.

### Branding Uniformity:

 Since the transparent logo is already part of the project extension, using it across both the web page and the extension ensures consistent branding and a unified visual identity.

### Responsive Design Compatibility:
 The transparent logo will ensure smoother integration into various sections of the website, especially when scaling or adapting for different devices.

## Steps to Reproduce:
Open the webpage in a browser.
Observe the non-transparent logo displayed on the page.
Note the visual inconsistency, especially on the dynamic or dark-colored backgrounds\.

## Expected Outcome:
The logo on the webpage should be updated to the transparent version, as already available in the project extension. This will improve visual flow, maintain uniformity, and enhance the professional appearance of the site.

issues.title: Transforming EduAid Home Page: Elevating UX, Accessibility and Interactivity
issues.body:
## Description:
Redesign the EduAid home page to reflect modern UX principles, creating a streamlined, visually appealing, and engaging user experience. The updated design will address multiple aspects of usability, accessibility, and performance:

### 1.Visual Design Enhancements: 
Introduced sleek hover effects, subtle animations, and a cohesive color palette to elevate the page's visual appeal. These updates not only improve the overall design but also make interactions more engaging and polished.

### 2.Accessibility Improvements: 
Prioritized inclusivity with the addition of alt text for all images, ensuring content is accessible for users with visual impairments. Also optimized contrast ratios for improved visibility and readability, and adjusted font sizes for better legibility across devices.

### 3.Responsive Layout: 
Streamlined the layout by reorganizing key feature buttons for intuitive navigation. The design is now fully responsive, providing a seamless user experience across desktops, tablets, and mobile devices.

### 4.Interactivity Enhancements: 
Integrated smooth scroll effects, dynamic button scaling on hover, and refined transitions to provide a more fluid, interactive experience that feels both modern and user-friendly.



## Benefits of the Revamp:

### Enhanced User Engagement: 
Animations and visual cues guide users naturally through the interface.
### Increased Accessibility: 
A more inclusive design ensures all users can easily navigate the platform.
### Professional Appearance:
 A polished, modern look boosts trust and credibility among users.
### Improved Usability:
 Clear navigation and intuitive design reduce friction, leading to a smoother user journey.

### This transformation will elevate EduAid to stand out as a professional, user-centric platform, attracting and retaining a wider audience. This update enhances not just the aesthetics but also the core functionality of the home page, making it more inclusive, engaging, and responsive.

issues.title: Refine entire UI of history page to ensure Consistent Visual Flow
issues.body:
## Description:

The History page UI needs refinement to ensure a polished, professional, and visually appealing user experience that aligns with the rest of the website. Key areas for improvement include:

### Header Section:
 Enhance logo size and center the "Edu Aid" text with proper flow.

### Content Layout:
 Improve quiz list presentation, ensuring proper spacing for interactivity. Style action buttons with consistent, smooth interactions.

### Spacing & Alignment:
 Ensure all sections have balanced spacing, proper alignment, and a responsive layout across all devices.

### Visual Consistency:
 Maintain cohesive design with consistent colors, fonts, and subtle appearance to elevate the page’s aesthetic.

## Goal:
To create a cohesive, engaging, and professional History page UI that integrates seamlessly with the overall website design, with improved usability and visual appeal.


## Expected Outcome:
By refining the entire History page UI, the result will be a more visually appealing, interactive, and consistent user experience that feels professional and polished. The improvements will help the page integrate seamlessly with the overall website design, providing a modern and cohesive look for users.

issues.title: Prevent Loading Without Input and Add Alert for Missing Data
issues.body:
# Description:

## Current Behavior:

The ***"Next"*** button initiates the loading process even when no input (text, file, or URL) is provided.

## Expected Behavior:

The ***"Next"*** button should validate the input fields and:
1. Display an alert if no input is provided.
2. Prevent the loading process from starting until valid input is given.

## Proposed Solution:

1. Add a validation check to ensure input is provided in one of the fields (text, file, or URL).
2. Display a concise alert: "Please provide input (text, file, or URL) to generate your quiz."
Stop execution and prevent loading if validation fails.

## Pros of the Fix:

1. Improves User Experience:

-Prevents confusion by guiding the user to provide necessary input.

2. Enhances Validation:

-Reduces unnecessary backend requests, optimizing system performance.

3. Error Prevention:

-Eliminates the risk of sending empty data to the backend.

4. Professional Workflow:

-Aligns the application behavior with expected user interaction patterns.

5. Efficiency:

-Saves user time by clearly communicating the issue upfront.

issues.title: Modify Difficulty Dropdown: Align Elements and Add missing 'Medium' Option on web.
issues.body: # Description ## Issue Summary: - The "Difficulty" dropdown required significant updates for better usability and clarity. Previously, the dropdown lacked a proper label, making it unclear what the dropdown represented. Additionally, the "Medium" option was missing, and the layout had the label above the dropdown instead of being side by side. ## Expected Behavior: - A proper "Difficulty" label should clearly describe the purpose of the dropdown. - The "Difficulty" label and dropdown should be displayed side by side for better visual alignment. - The "Medium" option should be available in the dropdown menu along with "Easy" and "Hard." ## Observed Behavior: - No label was present for the "Difficulty" dropdown, making its purpose unclear. - The "Difficulty" label appears above the dropdown. - The "Medium" option is missing from the dropdown menu. ## Present Page :
